lines.english: Some have gone to hell, and some yearn for paradise.
Worldly snares and entanglements of Maya,
egotism, attachment, doubt and loads of fear;
He winds up the drama, and then, O Nanak, He alone remains. ||7||
these came to be described in various ways.
He Himself creates and beholds His own drama.
pain and pleasure, honor and dishonor
Wherever the Eternal Lord's devotee is, He Himself is there.
He Himself is the Master of both worlds.
He unfolds the expanse of His creation for the glory of His Saint.
He Himself enjoys pleasures, and yet He is unaffected and untouched.
He Himself performs and plays His amusements and games.
His Praise is to Himself alone.
He is beyond calculation, beyond measure, uncountable and unfathomable.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਸੁ ਭਾਵੈ ਤਿਸੁ ਆਪਨ ਨਾਇ ਲਾਵੈ ॥
lines.translit: jis bhaavai tis aapan naae laavai |
lines.english: He attaches whomever He pleases to His Name.
He causes whomever He pleases to play in His play.
Salok:

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੀਅ ਜੰਤ ਕੇ ਠਾਕੁਰਾ ਆਪੇ ਵਰਤਣਹਾਰ ॥
lines.translit: jeea jant ke tthaakuraa aape varatanahaar |
lines.english: O Lord and Master of all beings and creatures, You Yourself are prevailing everywhere.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਉ ਬੁਲਾਵਹੁ ਤਿਉ ਨਾਨਕ ਦਾਸ ਬੋਲੈ ॥੮॥੨੧॥
lines.translit: jiau bulaavahu tiau naanak daas bolai |8|21|
lines.english: As You inspire him to speak, O Lord, so does servant Nanak speak. ||8||21||
He Himself is the speaker, and He Himself is the listener.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਏਕੋ ਪਸਰਿਆ ਦੂਜਾ ਕਹ ਦ੍ਰਿਸਟਾਰ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: naanak eko pasariaa doojaa keh drisattaar |1|
lines.english: O Nanak, The One is All-pervading; where is any other to be seen? ||1||
Ashtapadee:
He Himself is the One, and He Himself is the many.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਾ ਤਿਸੁ ਭਾਵੈ ਤਾ ਸ੍ਰਿਸਟਿ ਉਪਾਏ ॥
lines.translit: jaa tis bhaavai taa srisatt upaae |
lines.english: When it pleases Him, He creates the world.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੁਮ ਤੇ ਭਿੰਨ ਨਹੀ ਕਿਛੁ ਹੋਇ ॥
lines.translit: tum te bhin nahee kichh hoe |
lines.english: Without You, nothing can be done.
As He pleases, He absorbs it back into Himself.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਾ ਕਉ ਪ੍ਰਭ ਜੀਉ ਆਪਿ ਬੁਝਾਏ ॥
lines.translit: jaa kau prabh jeeo aap bujhaae |
lines.english: One whom God Himself inspires to understand
- that person obtains the True Name.
Upon Your thread, You have strung the whole world.
He looks impartially upon all, and he knows the essential reality.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੀਅ ਜੰਤ੍ਰ ਸਭ ਤਾ ਕੈ ਹਾਥ ॥
lines.translit: jeea jantr sabh taa kai haath |
lines.english: All beings and creatures are in His Hands.
O Nanak, he conquers the whole world. ||1||
One who is forgotten by God, is already dead.
He is Merciful to the meek, the Patron of the patronless.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਸੁ ਰਾਖੈ ਤਿਸੁ ਕੋਇ ਨ ਮਾਰੈ ॥
lines.translit: jis raakhai tis koe na maarai |
lines.english: No one can kill those who are protected by Him.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਿਸੁ ਤਜਿ ਅਵਰ ਕਹਾ ਕੋ ਜਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: tis taj avar kahaa ko jaae |
lines.english: Leaving Him, where else could anyone go?
Over the heads of all is the One, the Immaculate King.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੀਅ ਕੀ ਜੁਗਤਿ ਜਾ ਕੈ ਸਭ ਹਾਥਿ ॥
lines.translit: jeea kee jugat jaa kai sabh haath |
lines.english: The ways and means of all beings are in His Hands.
Slave Nanak is forever a sacrifice to Him. ||2||
Inwardly and outwardly, know that He is with you.
He is the Ocean of excellence, infinite and endless.
The Perfect, Merciful Lord is pervading everywhere.
He Himself knows His own ways.
His kindness extends to all.
The Inner-knower, the Searcher of hearts, is present everywhere.
Whoever pleases Him, He blends into Himself.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੋ ਜੋ ਰਚਿਓ ਸੁ ਤਿਸਹਿ ਧਿਆਤਿ ॥
lines.translit: jo jo rachio su tiseh dhiaat |
lines.english: That which He has created meditates on Him.
He cherishes His living beings in so many ways.
With heart-felt faith, they believe in Him.
O Nanak, they realize the One, the Creator Lord. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਭਗਤਿ ਕਰਹਿ ਹਰਿ ਕੇ ਗੁਣ ਗਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: bhagat kareh har ke gun gaae |
lines.english: They perform His devotional service and sing the Glorious Praises of the Lord.
The Lord's humble servant is committed to His Name.
The servant's purpose is to serve;

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਿਸ ਕੀ ਆਸ ਨ ਬਿਰਥੀ ਜਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: tis kee aas na birathee jaae |
lines.english: His hopes do not go in vain.
Within his mind, the Formless Lord abides.
obeying the Lord's Command, the supreme status is obtained.
Beyond this, he has no other thought.
Night and day, he worships the Feet of the Guru.
He is at peace in this world, and happy in the next.
His bonds are cut away, and he becomes free of hatred.
